explanation: The requirement says: When the user initiates a switch from PCV mode to PSV mode, the system must ask (i.e., require confirmation or parameter setting) for the PSV parameters. This means whenever 'PSVModeSelected' (i.e., user initiates the switch), 'confirmPSVParameters' (user confirms/sets PSV parameters) should immediately be required. The most direct encoding is to require that always, if PSVModeSelected occurs, confirmPSVParameters must occur in the next step. Template 11 encodes this: globally, if 'PSVModeSelected' then in the next step 'confirmPSVParameters' must hold.

The property expresses that whenever a user initiates a switch to PSV mode (represented by 'PSVModeSelected'), the system must eventually (within 10 time units) display a confirmation or setting of the PSV parameters (represented by 'confirmPSVParameters'). This captures the requirement that after the user begins a PCV-to-PSV switch, the system must prompt for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters. The choice of 'F[0,N_DURATION]' ensures this happens within a bounded time after the trigger event.

The requirement says that when the user initiates a switch from PCV mode to PSV mode, the system must ask the user to confirm or set the PSV parameters. Here, the atomic proposition 'PSVModeSelected' best represents 'a PCV-to-PSV switch is initiated by the user', and 'confirmPSVParameters' captures the system action to ask for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters. So the property should guarantee that whenever 'PSVModeSelected' is true, 'confirmPSVParameters' follows (i.e., is true at the same moment, or immediately after). Template 11 (G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3))) matches this: 'Whenever PSVModeSelected occurs, in the next step confirmPSVParameters occurs.' N_DURATION is not required for this template.

The requirement states that when the user initiates a switch from PCV to PSV mode, the system must ask the user to confirm or set the PSV ventilation parameters. The atomic proposition 'PSVModeSelected' matches the event of a user-initiated switch to PSV mode, and 'confirmPSVParameters' corresponds to a confirmation or setting of PSV parameters. The most direct and temporally aligned LTL template is 'G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3))', which specifies that globally, whenever the condition 'bool_exp2' (the user initiates a switch to PSV mode) becomes true, then 'bool_exp3' (the system asks for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters) must be true in the next state. No other atomic propositions are relevant according to the descriptions for the core requirement. N_DURATION is not needed with this template.

The property requires that whenever the user initiates a PCV-to-PSV mode switch (represented by atomic proposition PSVModeSelected), it must be followed (in the next step) by confirmation or setting of PSV parameters (represented by confirmPSVParameters). This directly encodes the requirement that upon a mode switch request, the system will ask the user for confirmation/settings before proceeding.

The input requirement states that when a user initiates a switch to Pressure Support Ventilation mode (PSV), the system must request confirmation and/or settings for the PSV parameters. In the provided atomic propositions, 'PSVModeSelected' indicates user initiation of the switch to PSV mode, and 'confirmPSVParameters' indicates the system's action to ask for confirmation of the PSV parameters. The appropriate LTL template to capture this cause-and-effect (trigger–response) pattern is: 'Globally, whenever PSVModeSelected becomes true, confirmPSVParameters must become true in the next state.' This is captured by 'G (PSVModeSelected -> (X confirmPSVParameters))'. This ensures that every time the user triggers a switch to PSV, the system immediately requests confirmation/settings for PSV parameters.

The requirement says that when a user initiates a switch from PCV to PSV, the system must request confirmation and/or setting of the required PSV parameters. This is a causality requirement: whenever the user initiates the switch to PSV mode (represented by the atomic proposition 'PSVModeSelected'), the system must (in the next step) ask for confirmation of PSV parameters ('confirmPSVParameters'). Template 11 expresses that whenever 'PSVModeSelected' holds, then in the next state 'confirmPSVParameters' must hold.

The natural language requirement states that when the user initiates a switch to PSV mode from PCV mode, the system must prompt the user for confirmation or setting of PSV parameters. Mapping this to the atomic propositions: 'PSVModeSelected' represents that the switch to PSV (from PCV) is initiated by the user, and 'confirmPSVParameters' indicates confirmation of the PSV parameters. Therefore, every time 'PSVModeSelected' becomes true, eventually 'confirmPSVParameters' must happen. This is best captured by a response template translating to: whenever PSVModeSelected is true, confirmPSVParameters must eventually occur. The template 'G (bool_exp2 -> F bool_exp3)' fits this causality. I set N_DURATION=1 as the specific timing is not given, so the basic eventuality is sufficient.

The requirement is that whenever the user initiates a switch from PCV to PSV mode (represented by 'PSVModeSelected'), the system must subsequently request confirmation or setting of PSV parameters (represented by 'confirmPSVParameters'). The most appropriate template is 'G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3))' (ID 11), which expresses: whenever 'PSVModeSelected' becomes true, in the next step, 'confirmPSVParameters' must also become true. This directly encodes the requirement that the confirmation dialog should appear (or parameter input should be prompted) immediately after initiating the switch.

The requirement states: when a user initiates a switch from PCV to PSV mode, the system must ask the user to confirm and/or set the PSV parameters to be used. This means that each time the PSVModeSelected atomic proposition becomes true (initiated by the user), the system shall trigger confirmPSVParameters, i.e., ask for confirmation or setting of the new mode parameters. To express this as LTL, we use the template where whenever PSVModeSelected occurs, in the next step confirmPSVParameters must hold (G (PSVModeSelected -> (X confirmPSVParameters))), ensuring the system responds to each user-initiated switch with the expected confirmation/setup action. This cleanly encodes the input requirement and matches the intent described.
